div1.entryFree:
Annoyance, 1) injury, harm: doing a. to the treacherous feet, R2 III, 2, 16. the herd hath more a. by the breese than by the tiger, Troil. I, 3, 48. remove from her the means of all a. Mcb. V, 1, 84.
2) that which harms or hurts: a grain, a dust, . . . . any a. in that precious sense, John IV, 1, 94. to souse a. that comes near his nest, V, 2, 150.
